rationale: The cited article is a methodological guide for probing interactions in moderated regression (spotlight/floodlight/simple-effects tests), and the citing paper reports extensive moderation and boundary-condition analyses across 14 studies. Given this clear methods overlap, the citation could be serving as an analysis/reporting source rather than a purely incidental mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no in-text snippet, it should be retained for second-stage judgment.
